# Questions in Livestream 215

Live Stream #215: Amalgamated Lamentations

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=742" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:22</a> | Will the narrator and all characters be voice-acted by both Doctor and Producer Falk? | He said only Doctor Falk would do the voice acting because Producer Falk did not want to. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=801" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:21</a> | Which pharaoh would have reigned during Abraham's visit to Egypt? | He said we do not know, though he places Abraham roughly in the 13th Dynasty and mentions rulers around Neferhotep I as plausible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=906" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:06</a> | Why were the king's reigns so short in the 13th Dynasty, and were they overthrown by military or common people? | He said we do not know; rotation and assassination theories have been floated, but the evidence does not settle it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=1031" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:11</a> | If reign lengths were so short, what prevented the dynasty from quickly running out of heirs? | He said that may indeed have been part of the instability, but the evidence is too thin to answer confidently. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=1090" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:10</a> | What is the etymology of Asher? | He said the etymology is not secure and discussed possible links with Asherah-type forms, Asserta, and Osiris without treating any option as settled. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=1301" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:41</a> | Is the Ethiopian church heretical because it accepts Enoch and some difficult ideas? | He said no, preferring to describe some features as problematic or aberrant rather than church-destroying heresy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=1627" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:07</a> | Are there restrictions on what counts as prayer in Christianity? | He said yes, because prayer can be misdirected, wrongly motivated, or undermined by persistent sinful posture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=1989" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:09</a> | How can a person of faith recognize a calling? | He said calling may appear as an unmistakable passion, as a kicking-and-screaming pressure from God, or may simply not be clear yet. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=2513" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:53</a> | Why were humans less central in Egyptian mythology than in Genesis? | He said Egyptian mythology is more centered on gods, kingship, and cosmic order than on humanity as the climax of creation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=2815" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:55</a> | Did Israelites ever manufacture weapons on a significant scale? | He said yes, especially in periods when northern kingdom wealth was tied to horses, chariots, and military trade. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=3265" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:25</a> | What is the difference between an M.Div. and a Th.M.? | He said the M.Div. is the broad pastoral and ministry degree, while the Th.M. is usually a more advanced research-oriented follow-up, with Dallas using the labels differently. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=3581" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:41</a> | Was Ramesses II less powerful than his reputation suggests? | He said yes, portraying Ramesses as an impressive builder whose military record and practical dominance are often exaggerated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=3901" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:01</a> | Was the Concordia seminary he mentioned St. Louis or Fort Wayne? | He said it was Fort Wayne. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=4004" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:44</a> | Was the Sermon on the Mount the same event as the Sermon on the Plain? | He said probably not the same event, though the overlap in content is unsurprising if Jesus repeated themes or if the Evangelists arranged similar teaching differently. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=4578" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:18</a> | Were the Hyksos Asiatic or Palestinian in origin? | He said they were Levantine Asiatic peoples, especially associated with Amorite-type backgrounds, not Palestinian in either the Philistine or modern Arab sense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=4886" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:26</a> | Is there a big difference between East and West Coast Canadian accents? | He said yes, contrasting Newfoundland speech with Vancouver and noting that Vancouver sounds much closer to the U.S. West Coast. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=5428" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:28</a> | What is your favorite ancient Near Eastern idiom? | He highlighted idioms such as cutting a covenant and the Mizpah formula that works more like a threat than a sentimental blessing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=5910" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:30</a> | Why not make YouTube Shorts to attract more viewers? | He said shorts require a lot of work, have diminishing returns, and YouTube is no longer pushing them the way it once did. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=6529" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:49</a> | Are bad reviews acceptable? | He said yes, because honest mixed or negative reviews make the overall review profile look more trustworthy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=6683" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:23</a> | Is Amazon the best place to get your books? | He said it is a good option, partly because Amazon's recommendation system helps discovery even if his direct financial return is similar elsewhere. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=6760" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:40</a> | Is there a connection between the number of the beast and Solomon? | He said yes, reading 666 against Solomon's sudden concentrated wealth as part of the symbolic background for the beast figure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=7763" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:23</a> | Is it worth learning Hebrew to read the Bible in the original? | He said absolutely, because it removes one layer of obfuscation even though exegesis is still required. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=7830" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:30</a> | What Bible translation is best overall? | He said he often uses the NASB 1977 for apologetics and especially likes the CSB as a balanced general choice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=8003" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:23</a> | Are Bible translations shaped by commercial motives? | He said yes, because publishers often profit from the translation itself and from the broader ecosystem of study materials built on it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=8148" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:48</a> | If someone quotes the NIV in a public article, will they get sued? | He said no for a single quote or a couple of lines, but longer use in books can run into translation licensing limits. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=8406" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:06</a> | What are some ways to improve D&D? | He said he would rather go back a few editions and allow more imaginative freedom than what he sees in the current direction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=8576" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:56</a> | Weren't orcs supposed to be the evil faction originally in D&D? | He said yes, in older D&D they were evil, but they have since been rehabilitated in strange ways. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=8620" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:40</a> | Is Paul Overland credible? | He said he does not know the person and therefore cannot judge. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=8665" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:25</a> | What is your take on the Tower of Babel in Genesis 11:1-9? | He treated it as mythohistory, meaning a historical core is being interpreted through a theological and literary frame. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=8919" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:39</a> | Does mythohistory mean people take history and ask what the event says about spiritual reality? | He said that is broadly the idea: mythohistory relates a historical core to a culture's understanding of divine or spiritual meaning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=8994" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:54</a> | Did pharaohs believe they would reincarnate into other earthly bodies after death? | He said no, because Egyptian afterlife belief is better described as cyclical continuation in the next world rather than reincarnation into new earthly bodies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=9040" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:40</a> | Does Freemasonry come from ancient Egypt or Solomon's temple? | He said no; despite Masonic claims, he traces it to European guild movements rather than ancient Egypt or Solomon's temple. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=9188" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:08</a> | How did Masons turn into what they are now if they began as ordinary guilds? | He said the decline of cathedral-building work left stone-cutting guilds to become broader employment and networking organizations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=9397" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:37</a> | Can someone be both a Christian and a Mason? | He said no, because Masonry allows multiple paths to God and is therefore incompatible with Christian exclusivity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=9703" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:43</a> | Could Freemasonry be rooted in Satan worship? | He said no, locating its roots more in Hermetic and occult currents than in explicit Satanism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=10197" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:57</a> | If being a Freemason is supposed to be secret, could someone get in trouble for talking openly about membership? | He said generally no; they recruit openly, though they do not disclose the deeper secrets to outsiders. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=10319" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:59</a> | Can anyone join Freemasonry, or do they prefer certain people? | He said they tend to be selective and somewhat elitist rather than universally open. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=10501" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:01</a> | Is Genesis 2:4 a recap chiasm of Genesis 1 or a different creation story? | He said neither, preferring to read it as a toledot-style transition that introduces the garden narrative rather than a second independent creation account. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=10903" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:01:43</a> | How is AI in archaeology or Dead Sea Scroll work different from earlier computer analysis? | He said earlier computer methods already did important work, and current AI mostly helps adjudicate between other lines of evidence rather than replacing them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=11315" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:35</a> | What kind of sauce works best with pasta? | He said it depends on the pasta and mentions options like carbonara, butter-garlic, Alfredo, and tomato sauces. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=11461" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:11:01</a> | Are Pop-Tarts a ravioli? | He said no, because Pop-Tarts are pastries and are not boiled. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=11519" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:11:59</a> | Are breakfast dessert pizzas a yes or no? | He said it depends, and he has had good apple strudel and strawberry dessert pizzas. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=11890" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:10</a> | Which era of Saturday morning cartoons did you grow up with? | He said mostly the 1970s and early 1980s. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=12220" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:40</a> | Why was Israel technologically behind its neighbors? Was that so it would rely on God? | He rejected the premise, saying Israel was a pastoral society and not uniquely backward in the simplistic way the question suggests. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=12341" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:41</a> | Do we really know how ancient Egyptian music sounded? | He said no, since proposed scales exist but the actual tuning, notation, and sound remain uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=12832" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:52</a> | How can students avoid brain rot and procrastination? | He said the two problems differ, with procrastination often tied to depression and brain rot tied to intellectual passivity, so the remedy is more real reading, thinking, and active engagement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=13861" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:01</a> | When starting a new topic, should I buy one book or many? | He said to begin with one strong overview book and then follow its bibliography outward. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/LYoh2N5SRJQ?t=14038" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:53:58</a> | Is there a good way to know whether a book has a useful bibliography before buying it? | He said yes, by using previews or library access and then judging whether the citations are current and substantial. |
